mandate: Close codex §9 [P2]: SDK-authored out-of-window nodes leak through Engine::search. Part 1 = apply the default validity predicate on the search hydration path. Part 2 = thread ReadView through search as an optional argument, both bindings, X1 parity.
status: COMPLETE — both parts done. No blockers. Two decisions owed to HITL (below).

commits.subject: test(0.8.20): RED — Slice 15b fix-2, validity window must govern search
fix(0.8.20): GREEN — validity window governs every search hydration site
feat(0.8.20): Slice 15b fix-2 Part 2 — ReadView on search across Py + TS
commits.touches: fathomdb-engine, fathomdb-py, fathomdb-napi, python/fathomdb/{engine.py,_fathomdb.pyi}, python/tests, ts/src/{index.ts,binding.ts}, ts/tests, dev/interfaces/{rust,python,typescript}.md, dev/DOC-INDEX.md

leak_test_evidence.method: Temporary probe test on the UNMODIFIED engine, using only the shipped public API (no new methods), then deleted. The suite's own RED state was a compile error (it calls the not-yet-existing search_view), which alone would NOT have proven the leak — so the runtime reproduction was done separately and deliberately.
leak_test_evidence.probe_input: engine.write([node(logical_id=EXPIRED, body='quarterly telemetry report', valid_until=2000), node(logical_id=ALWAYS, body='quarterly telemetry summary', no window)]); drain; engine.search('telemetry')
leak_test_evidence.verdict: LEAK CONFIRMED. valid_until = 2000 (i.e. 1970) is decades in the past; read_get/read_list correctly hid the node while search returned it. After the fix the same input returns only the unbounded sibling.
leak_test_evidence.green_after_fix: cargo test -p fathomdb-engine --test slice15b_search_validity -> exit 0, 8 passed / 0 failed

sites_patched: read_search_in_tx vector branch — canonical_nodes lookup by write_cursor (:now bound at ?2)
read_search_in_tx text branch — search_index LEFT JOIN canonical_nodes cn (:now bound at ?2)
bfs_graph_arm_candidates — entity-node FTS seed (:now bound at ?3, after ?1 MATCH / ?2 LIMIT)
bfs_graph_arm_candidates — seed resolution active_stmt (:now bound at ?2)
bfs_graph_arm_candidates — BFS neighbour hydration body_stmt (:now bound at ?2)
part_1_the_fix.graph_arm_note: The three graph-arm sites were NOT named in the codex finding (which cited read_search_in_tx only) but are the SAME defect class, reachable from the governed surface via search_reranked(.., use_graph_arm = true). Fixing only read_search_in_tx would have left search_reranked leaking. Flagging as a deliberate, slightly-wider-than-cited scope.
part_1_the_fix.predicate_reuse: Every site calls ReadView::validity_sql(alias, now_idx). ZERO hand-rolled copies of the predicate — verified by grep: no site emits valid_from/valid_until comparison SQL of its own. Reuse was possible at all five sites; no STOP-and-report was needed.
part_1_the_fix.now_seam: ReadView::now_param() is called ONCE per query in read_search_in_tx and ONCE per call in bfs_graph_arm_candidates, in Rust, then bound positionally. Confirmed by grep that the fix introduces no datetime('now') and no strftime('%s','now') anywhere. It binds once per query, not per row (the per-hit vector lookup rebinds the SAME precomputed i64).
part_1_the_fix.left_untouched_deliberately: The pre-step-12 and pre-step-8 fallback statements in the text branch. valid_from/valid_until arrive in schema step 22, so those schemas cannot hold a window and a predicate there would be a compile error at SQL-prepare time. Comment added recording this.

part_2_the_escape_hatch.why_not_a_signature_change: The prompt said to STOP if threading ReadView forced a signature change across many call sites. It WOULD have: measured 181 call sites of the six public search entry points (.search( alone = 125). So the six shipped signatures were left BYTE-UNCHANGED and the view rides the PRIVATE choke point instead (search_reranked_with_explain -> search_inner -> search_inner_with_stats -> ReaderRequest::Search -> read_search_in_tx). Three ADDITIVE public methods carry the view. This is a deviation from Slice 10's literal plumbing (which made view a REQUIRED &ReadView param on the read verbs) and is the reason I did not stop: the additive route achieves the same caller-visible outcome at zero call-site churn. Flagging it explicitly so the reviewer can overrule.
new_rust_methods: Engine::search_reranked_view(query, filter, rerank_depth, use_graph_arm, alpha, pool_n, explain, &ReadView)
part_2_the_escape_hatch.python: engine.search(..., view: ReadView | None = None) and engine.search_text_only(query, view=None). Reuses fathomdb.types.ReadView (no new type) and read._to_native_view (no duplicated translation). Not circular at runtime: fathomdb.read imports fathomdb.engine only under TYPE_CHECKING.
part_2_the_escape_hatch.typescript: engine.search(..., view?: ReadView) and engine.searchTextOnly(query, view?). Reuses the exported ReadView interface; mirrors the read verbs' trailing options object.
part_2_the_escape_hatch.binding_ts_note: src/ts/src/binding.ts is a HAND-WRITTEN mirror of the native surface and had to be updated too — the generated src/ts/index.d.ts is gitignored (.gitignore:46) and regenerates correctly, but tsc types #native from binding.ts. Without the binding.ts edit the TS build fails 'Expected 1-7 arguments, but got 8'. Not mentioned in the prompt; worth knowing for future binding work.

no_regression_proof.claim: Adding the predicate to the DEFAULT search path does not change results on any corpus that never authored a window.
no_regression_proof.mechanism: validity_sql emits '(alias.valid_from IS NULL OR alias.valid_from <= ?n) AND (alias.valid_until IS NULL OR alias.valid_until > ?n)'. Schema step 22 added both columns as nullable INTEGER with NO DEFAULT and back-filled NULL, so both disjuncts are TRUE for every pre-existing row.
no_regression_proof.premise_asserted_not_assumed: default_search_is_unchanged_on_a_corpus_with_no_authored_windows reads canonical_nodes DIRECTLY and asserts (valid_from, valid_until) == (NULL, NULL) for every seeded row BEFORE asserting search equality — so the no-op argument rests on a measured premise, not a claim.
no_regression_proof.empirical_proof: cargo test -p fathomdb-engine -p fathomdb-schema -- --test-threads=1 => 557 passed / 0 failed / 13 ignored. Baseline was 549 passed / 0 failed. Delta is +8, which is EXACTLY the 8 tests added in the RED commit. Every changed count is accounted for: no pre-existing test changed state, none was adjusted, none was deleted or re-ignored.
no_regression_proof.ts_accounting: 201 pass / 0 fail vs baseline 194 pass / 0 fail. Delta +7 = exactly the 7 new TS tests. No pre-existing TS test changed state.
no_regression_proof.r20rv_guard: default_read_view_is_behavior_identical (the guard the prompt named as the tripwire for this class of change) is in slice10_read_view.rs and PASSES unchanged.
no_regression_proof.left_join_semantics_preserved: The text branch deliberately KEEPS ownerless search_index rows (no matching cn) via its LEFT JOIN and explicit 'OR cn.state IS NULL'. validity_sql is NULL-permissive by construction, so an ownerless row reads NULL on both validity columns and is still kept. No extra 'OR IS NULL' was added — that would have been a second, drifting copy of the predicate.

decisions_owed_to_hitl.topic: Axis scope of ReadView on search — I chose REFUSE, and it is arguable.
decisions_owed_to_hitl.what_i_did: search honours the VALIDITY axis (valid_as_of, include_out_of_window) and REFUSES the EXISTENCE axis (include_superseded, include_inactive) with a typed EngineError::InvalidArgument.
decisions_owed_to_hitl.why: Silently ignoring them would be exactly the dead/incoherent surface this fix exists to remove. Fully honouring them would mean relaxing 'superseded_at IS NULL' on a retrieval path, which resurrects the stale-body leak the Slice-15 fix-1 review closed; and search hydrates from projection indexes (search_index, vector_default) that are not version-complete, so 'include superseded' has no truthful answer there. Also, the text branch's existence predicate is deliberately 'cn.superseded_at IS NULL AND (cn.state = active OR cn.state IS NULL)' — the OR-IS-NULL keeps ownerless rows — so ReadView::existence_sql could NOT have been reused verbatim without changing that behaviour, which the hard no-regression requirement forbids.
decisions_owed_to_hitl.alternatives: (a) silently ignore, (b) fully honour on the vector branch only, (c) honour by routing through a version-complete source. All are defensible; I picked the smallest coherent one.
decisions_owed_to_hitl.reversibility: Cheap. It is one guard function (ReadView::reject_existence_relaxation_on_search) called from two places, plus one Rust test and one Py test asserting the refusal.

decisions_owed_to_hitl.topic: Additive methods instead of Slice-10-style signature change.
decisions_owed_to_hitl.what_i_did: Left the six shipped search signatures byte-unchanged and added search_view / search_reranked_view / search_text_only_view.
decisions_owed_to_hitl.why: 181 measured call sites. The prompt told me to STOP before forcing a signature change across many call sites; the additive route reaches the same caller-visible outcome without one, so I proceeded rather than blocking.
decisions_owed_to_hitl.cost: Three new Rust methods rather than one uniform convention — a mild divergence from how the five read verbs look. If uniformity is preferred over call-site churn, this should be re-done as a signature change and is a mechanical (if wide) edit.

unverified.reason: The native module cannot be built from this worktree, and src/python/tests/_test_hooks_gate.py is a structural guard which I did NOT defeat.
unverified.what_was_done_instead: ruff check clean; python -m py_compile clean on both engine.py and the new test module; _fathomdb.pyi updated with the view parameter on search AND with a search_text_only stub that was MISSING entirely at baseline.
unverified.needs: The orchestrator's fresh-clone run. Reference baseline was 800 passed / 12 skipped, exit 0. Expect +7 tests (the new module has 6 test functions, one of which is parametrized 2 ways = 7 collected) => expect 807 passed / 12 skipped.

unverified.item: Whether the Python view= kwarg actually reaches PyO3 at runtime
unverified.detail: cargo check confirms the pyo3 signature accepts view=None and that the types line up, but the Python-side keyword plumbing (engine.py -> _to_native_view -> native search(view=...)) is only statically checked. This is the highest-value thing for the fresh-clone run to confirm.
unverified.mitigation: The equivalent TS path IS execution-verified (201/201), and the two bindings are structurally parallel.

things_this_prompt_or_the_docs_got_wrong.detail: Read it directly. It does specify ReadView as 'A single optional arg (an options-object / kwargs — never positional) on search, read.get, read.list, graph.neighbors'. The prompt's citation holds. Worth noting the doc explicitly says never positional, which is why the additive Rust methods + optional kwarg/options-object in the bindings is a faithful lowering.
549 + 8 new = 557 measured. The prompt's baseline was accurate.

things_this_prompt_or_the_docs_got_wrong.item: The codex finding named only read_search_in_tx — INCOMPLETE, not wrong.
things_this_prompt_or_the_docs_got_wrong.detail: The same defect class exists at three further node-hydration sites inside bfs_graph_arm_candidates (the opt-in graph arm), reachable from the governed surface via search_reranked(use_graph_arm=true). Fixing only the cited function would have shipped a partial fix. All three are now patched.

things_this_prompt_or_the_docs_got_wrong.item: The prompt did not mention src/ts/src/binding.ts.
things_this_prompt_or_the_docs_got_wrong.detail: It is a hand-written mirror of the native surface and MUST be updated alongside any napi signature change; the generated index.d.ts being gitignored makes this easy to miss. Failure mode is a tsc arity error that looks like a code defect.

things_this_prompt_or_the_docs_got_wrong.item: _fathomdb.pyi had NO search_text_only stub at baseline.
things_this_prompt_or_the_docs_got_wrong.detail: Pre-existing gap, not one this fix created. Added while updating the stub for view=.
